question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `token.id` with `gt` operator (not `gte`) combined with a `serial.number` lower bound to trigger `validateLowerBounds()` error, and use the error response timing to perform a side-channel enumeration of valid token IDs in NFT transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply both `spender.id=gt:X` and `spender.id=lt:Y` (one of each type) to pass `validateFilters()` and construct a range-based spender filter that returns NFT approval transactions for all spenders between X and Y, leaking cross-account transaction approval data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `timestamp` filter with a very large value (near `maxLong`) to `extractStakingRewardsQuery()` to cause integer overflow or unexpected behavior in the SQL parameter binding, altering which staking reward transaction records are returned?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: extractNftMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `spender.id=lte:X` and `spender.id=gte:Y` (where Y > X) to pass `validateFilters()` (each type appears only once) but create an impossible range condition in the SQL query, causing the database to return zero NFT transaction records and masking legitimate approval data?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/accountController.js -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `timestamp=lte:X` filter where X is the current time to `extractStakingRewardsQuery()`, combined with `order=asc` and maximum `limit`, to retrieve all staking reward transactions for any account from the beginning of time, leaking complete financial transaction history?
